Title: Enrichment of tomato fruit with health-promoting anthocyanins by expression of select transcription factors

Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: fruit-specific transgenic expression under E8 promoter; stable inheritance across generations; metabolite profiling by spectrophotometry and HPLC; suppression subtractive hybridization; northern blot; microarray; PAL enzyme assay; antioxidant capacity assay
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern: expressed in ripening fruit, visible pigmentation beginning at end of mature green stage and intensifying after onset consistent with E8 promoter activity
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: functions together with Ros1 as a MYB-bHLH regulatory combination
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: PAL; 4CL; C4H; C3H; CHS; CHI; F3H; F3'H; F3'5'H; DFR; ANS; 3-GT; RT; 5-GT; putative anthocyanin acyltransferase; GST; putative anthocyanin transporter
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates expression of almost all anthocyanin biosynthetic genes and genes for anthocyanin side-chain modification and vacuolar transport; strongly induces PAL and F3H expression
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: anthocyanin biosynthesis, side-chain modification, and vacuolar transport/sequestration
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: fruit ripening context via E8 promoter
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: hydrophilic antioxidant activity; associated with protection against cancer, cardiovascular disease, age-related degenerative disease, inflammation, visual decline, obesity and diabetes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes high anthocyanin accumulation and intense purple pigmentation in tomato fruit, increasing hydrophilic antioxidant capacity
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: essential activator in the successful MYB-bHLH combination that enabled unusually high anthocyanin accumulation in tomato fruit
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: high-value engineering regulator for biofortifying tomato and potentially other crops with anthocyanins; best deployed with Ros1 and fruit-specific promoters to target edible tissues while avoiding vegetative effects
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: no obvious abnormal vegetative phenotype reported; off-target increase in peel flavonols also observed
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Del alone was not the subject of direct single-gene testing here; effects were demonstrated in combination with Ros1. The transgene combination was transferred into Money Maker, Ailsa Craig and VF36 backgrounds without loss of phenotype
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: suppression subtractive hybridization; tomato oligonucleotide microarray; ArrayExpress E-MEXP-1830, array platform A-MEXP-1417
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Fruit-specific expression of snapdragon Del together with Ros1 induced strong purple pigmentation in tomato peel and flesh. 2. The Del/Ros1 combination activated nearly the full anthocyanin pathway, including PAL, F3H and F3'5'H, as well as transport/modification genes. 3. This regulatory activation led to up to about 2.83 mg anthocyanin per g fresh weight and increased hydrophilic antioxidant capacity about threefold.
